U1.name: Cluster Version and Support Status
U1.description: Check current cluster version, support status, and whether extended support pricing applies

U2.name: Target Version Availability
U2.description: Validate the target Kubernetes version is available and is the next sequential minor version

U3.name: Multi-Version Hop Detection
U3.description: Detect if the upgrade requires multiple sequential version hops (EKS only supports one minor version at a time)

U4.name: EKS Upgrade Insights
U4.description: Retrieve and analyze AWS EKS upgrade readiness insights including deprecated API usage, addon compatibility, and cluster health

U5.name: EKS Managed Addon Compatibility
U5.description: Check installed EKS managed addon versions against target Kubernetes version compatibility

U6.name: Self-Managed Addon Detection
U6.description: Detect core addons (CoreDNS, kube-proxy, VPC CNI) installed outside the EKS addon system that won't be checked by Upgrade Insights

U7.name: Addon Health Issues
U7.description: Check for any health issues reported by installed EKS addons that could impact upgrade

U8.name: Node Kubelet Version Skew
U8.description: Check kubelet versions across all nodes to ensure compliance with Kubernetes version skew policy after upgrade

U9.name: Node Group AMI Type
U9.description: Check managed node group AMI types for AL2 deprecation (deprecated in 1.32, removed in 1.33+)

U10.name: Node Group Pending Updates
U10.description: Check for pending launch template updates on managed node groups that could compound with the upgrade

U11.name: Subnet IP Availability
U11.description: Check if cluster subnets have sufficient available IPs for control plane ENIs during upgrade (minimum 5 per subnet required)

U12.name: PodDisruptionBudget Coverage
U12.description: Check if production workloads have PodDisruptionBudgets to protect availability during node rolling updates

U13.name: Single-Replica Workloads
U13.description: Identify Deployments and StatefulSets with only 1 replica that will have guaranteed downtime during node replacement

U14.name: Missing Readiness Probes
U14.description: Check for containers without readiness probes that could receive traffic before being ready after node replacement

U15.name: Pod Topology and Anti-Affinity
U15.description: Check for workloads missing topology spread constraints or pod anti-affinity that risk all replicas on the same node

U16.name: MinReadySeconds Configuration
U16.description: Check if StatefulSets have minReadySeconds > 0 to prevent premature readiness during rolling updates

U17.name: TerminationGracePeriodSeconds Zero
U17.description: Check for StatefulSets with terminationGracePeriodSeconds set to 0 which is unsafe during upgrades

U18.name: Cluster Autoscaler Version Compatibility
U18.description: Check if Cluster Autoscaler version matches the target Kubernetes version

U19.name: Karpenter Version Compatibility
U19.description: Check Karpenter deployment version for compatibility with target Kubernetes version

U20.name: Third-Party Controller Inventory
U20.description: Scan for known third-party controllers (cert-manager, Istio, ArgoCD, external-secrets, etc.) and report their versions for manual compatibility review

U21.name: Control Plane Logging
U21.description: Check if control plane audit logging is enabled (required for Upgrade Insights to detect deprecated API usage)

U22.name: Cluster Health Issues
U22.description: Check for any health issues reported by the EKS control plane that could block upgrade

U23.name: kube-proxy Version Skew
U23.description: Check kube-proxy version to ensure it won't exceed the 3 minor version skew limit after upgrade

U24.name: Deprecated APIs in Live Resources
U24.description: Scan CRDs, webhooks, FlowSchemas, and other resources for deprecated API versions that will be removed in the target version

U25.name: Deprecated APIs in Helm Releases
U25.description: Decode Helm release secrets and scan stored manifests for deprecated API versions not visible through the live API

U26.name: Deprecated APIs Warning (Future Versions)
U26.description: Identify resources using API versions that are deprecated but not yet removed in the target version (proactive warning)

U27.name: Node Subnet IP Availability
U27.description: Check if node subnets have sufficient IPs for node rolling replacement during upgrade

U28.name: Pod Subnet IP Availability (Custom Networking)
U28.description: Check pod subnet IPs when VPC CNI custom networking is enabled (pods use different subnets than nodes)

U29.name: EC2 Instance Service Limits
U29.description: Check if EC2 service quotas allow launching additional instances during node group rolling updates

U30.name: EBS GP2 Volume Service Limits
U30.description: Check EBS GP2 volume service quotas for PV re-attachment during node replacement

U31.name: EBS GP3 Volume Service Limits
U31.description: Check EBS GP3 volume service quotas for PV re-attachment during node replacement

U32.name: Self-Managed Node Group Launch Template Updates
U32.description: Check for self-managed node groups with pending launch template updates

U33.name: kube-proxy IPVS Mode Deprecation
U33.description: Check if kube-proxy uses IPVS mode which is deprecated in K8s 1.35+ and removed in 1.36

U34.name: Ingress NGINX Controller Retirement
U34.description: Check for the retired Kubernetes community Ingress NGINX controller that should be migrated

U35.name: Docker Socket Mounts
U35.description: Check for pods mounting docker.sock or dockershim.sock which do not exist on containerd-based EKS nodes (containerd is the only runtime since K8s 1.24)

U36.name: Workloads Scaled to Zero
U36.description: Check for workloads scaled to zero replicas that may be forgotten during upgrade validation

U37.name: EKS Configuration Insights
U37.description: Retrieve EKS MISCONFIGURATION insights that may affect cluster health during upgrade

U38.name: Third-Party API Deprecations
U38.description: Check for deprecated API versions specific to third-party components (Istio, cert-manager) from the deprecation database
